asyncAnsweringQuestions: Answering student questions (based on your profile).

background.description: Let's get to know you better! Please fill out your background information so others in the MindHive community can connect with you more easily.

officialBio.description: In ~100 words, describe your expertise, experience, and educational background for a high school audience.
officialBio.title: Official Bio

background.title: Background

unofficialBio.description: In a couple of sentences, describe your journey leading to where you are now, professionally. And look ahead to the next 5 years or beyond. The goal is for students to understand that professional lives are not always a straight path and that there are coincidences, good luck and bad luck, and sometimes multiple career paths hidden behind official bios you might read on a scientist's website.
unofficialBio.title: Unofficial Bio

basic.description: Please complete the following items to create your profile and connect with the MindHive community.
basic.subtitle: Your basic information helps the MindHive community get to know you better. This includes who you are, where you're located, and what you look like.
basic.title: Basic Information

connectSubtitle: Search for, discover, and connect with the MindHive community

organization.intro1: To ensure a successful capstone experience, each sponsoring organization must designate a primary sponsor who will serve as a stakeholder and point of contact for the student team. The designated sponsor will be the key liaison for all project-related communications.
organization.intro2: Capstone projects are most successful when sponsors provide high-level strategic guidance while mentors offer day-to-day insights and support. Your involvement is essential to both student learning and project success.
organization.leadAvailabilityDescription: Please estimate a numerical time per week or per month. Refrain from answering "meet when available" or similar response.
organization.title: Point of Contact

description.individual: Please complete the following items to create your profile and connect with the MindHive community.
description.organization: Please complete the following items to create your organization's profile and connect with the MindHive community.

organizationFields.locationDescription: Please list the city and state/country where you'll be operating from.
organizationFields.missionDescription: Please describe your organization or unit including mission, key program activities, and nature of work.

description.individual: What topics would you like to support students with? Select some topics and areas of interest from the list below to let other community members know where you're excited to contribute. This will help us connect you with students and peers who share your passions.
description.organization: What topics would you like your organization to support students with? Select some topics and areas of interest from the list below to let other community members know where you're excited to contribute. This will help us connect you with students and peers who share your passions.

createProfileFlow.saveError: Something went wrong while saving. Please try again.

sponsorMentor.additionalNotes: Is there any additional information you would like to highlight?
sponsorMentor.backgroundMethodologyDescription: Please include any important contextual information related to your project, and outline your methodologies including specific research approaches, data collection techniques, tools, and technologies.
sponsorMentor.categorizationDescription: Which category does your project align with?
sponsorMentor.competenciesDescription: List any specific skills or qualifications that would be useful for students to have in order to successfully address this problem and complete the capstone project. For example, user experience design, software programming, economics/financial analysis, public policy analysis, quantitative research/analysis, engineering skills (mechanical, electrical, etc). Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.dataRequirements: What are the anticipated data requirements for the project, and how will each required data resource be provided to the capstone team?
sponsorMentor.dataRequirementsDescription: Please describe what datasets will be used. For example, existing source data will be provided by the sponsor, existing source data is free and open (public), etc. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.dataSecurityConcerns: Does the sponsor have any anticipated data security concerns to be cleared for student team?
sponsorMentor.dataSecurityNotes: If yes or maybe, please describe concerns.
sponsorMentor.fieldWorkLikelihood: How likely would the project require field work or site visits (either within NYC or outside)?
sponsorMentor.guidelinesDescription: I have read and understood the Capstone proposal guidelines in full, including all of the Capstone Sponsor FAQs and Mutual Expectations agreement and agree to abide by them.
sponsorMentor.guidelinesRequestAppointment: I request an appointment to discuss further.
sponsorMentor.intro1: Sponsors who wish to take on a more hands-on role in guiding students can indicate their interest by selecting the checkbox below. By doing so, they acknowledge their understanding of the mentor's responsibilities and time commitment. As a mentor, sponsors will provide students with regular feedback, subject matter expertise, and practical insights to help them navigate project challenges and refine their work.
sponsorMentor.intro2: This role requires active engagement throughout the capstone experience, ensuring students receive the support needed to produce meaningful outcomes. If selected, sponsors will serve as both the primary stakeholder and a mentor, bridging strategic direction with day-to-day guidance.
sponsorMentor.learningOutcomesDescription: Provide 2-3 learning outcomes or expected deliverables. For example, an interactive dashboard or a website showcasing data analysis outcomes. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.projectAbstractDescription: 100 words maximum. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.projectDescriptionDescription: 250 words maximum. Describe the scope and nature of the capstone project. Please ensure that the project is achievable within the academic timeframe and with the resources available. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.projectTitleDescription: 15 words maximum. Tips: Be engaging for student appeal
sponsorMentor.relevanceDescription: Describe why this issue is of relevance to CUSP's mission and urban science.
sponsorMentor.relevantLinksDescription: This can include a project website, GitHub repository, or other online presence where one can learn more about the project. We'll include QR codes in the brochure.
sponsorMentor.researchQuestion: What is the research question/scope to be explored OR what is the problem that you want to address?
sponsorMentor.sponsorIsMentor: I understand the time commitment and intend to serve as the sponsor AND mentor.
sponsorMentor.techRequirements: Does the sponsor have technology infrastructure and/or software application requirements for this project?
sponsorMentor.techRequirementsDescription: For example, the project requires GIS data analysis and the sponsor uses ArcGIS or RShiny for mapping. If the sponsor has no preferences as to data tools used, please indicate.
sponsorMentor.title: Sponsor & Mentor

createProfileFlow.unsavedChangesWarning: Your unsaved changes will be lost. Click Cancel to return and save your changes.

interests.description: What topics would you like to support students with? Select some topics and areas of interest from the list below to let other community members know where you're excited to contribute. This will help us connect you with students and peers who share your passions.
interests.title: Where can you help?

interestSelector.placeholder: Begin typing to search for interests

introduction.description: We'd love for you to upload an introduction video to share with students and fellow MindHive members. You can use it to share a project you think students might get excited about.

passion.description: For example, you can briefly describe a science project you are working on. Or you can tell us about your experience bringing science to the public. This is your space to showcase what you're currently working on and how it shapes the expertise you bring to our community.
passion.title: What are you passionate about right now?

introduction.title: Introduction Video

matchingRound.notAvailableYet: This round is not available yet. Your teacher is still setting it up.

myOpportunitiesList.deleteConfirm: Delete this opportunity? This cannot be undone.
myOpportunitiesList.empty: You haven't created any opportunities yet. Click New opportunity to publish your first project for students.
myOpportunitiesList.emptyFiltered: No opportunities match the current filters.
myOpportunitiesList.reviewCommentsWaiting: Teacher review comments are waiting for you.
myOpportunitiesList.statusChangeUseEditor: Complete required fields in the editor before submitting for review.

opportunityEditor.additionalNotes: Is there any additional information you would like to highlight?
opportunityEditor.backgroundMethodologyDescription: Please include any important contextual information related to your project, and outline your methodologies including specific research approaches, data collection techniques, tools, and technologies.
opportunityEditor.categorizationDescription: Which category does your project align with?
opportunityEditor.classNetworksEmptyWarning: Teachers will not see this opportunity in their review queue until you select at least one class network.
opportunityEditor.classNetworksHint: Select one or more class networks you belong to. Teachers in those networks can review this opportunity.
opportunityEditor.classNetworksNoMembership: You are not a member of any class networks yet.
opportunityEditor.competenciesDescription: List any specific skills or qualifications that would be useful for students to have in order to successfully address this problem and complete the capstone project. For example, user experience design, software programming, economics/financial analysis, public policy analysis, quantitative research/analysis, engineering skills (mechanical, electrical, etc). Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
opportunityEditor.coverImageHint: Upload a file (stored on our cloud bucket) or paste a direct image URL below. Uploaded files take priority if both are set.
opportunityEditor.coverImageUrlHint: Or paste a URL (max {{maxMb}} MB for uploads):

customQuestions.description: Optional questions students answer when they rank this opportunity. Used by the matching algorithm.
customQuestions.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Why are you interested in this project?
customQuestions.saveFirst: Save the opportunity first, then come back here to add custom questions.
customQuestions.title: Custom application questions

opportunityEditor.dataRequirements: What are the anticipated data requirements for the project, and how will each required data resource be provided to the capstone team?
opportunityEditor.dataRequirementsDescription: Please describe what datasets will be used. For example, existing source data will be provided by the sponsor, existing source data is free and open (public), etc. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
opportunityEditor.dataSecurityConcerns: Does the sponsor have any anticipated data security concerns to be cleared for student team?
opportunityEditor.dataSecurityNotes: If yes or maybe, please describe concerns.
opportunityEditor.descriptionPlaceholder: What will students do? What will they learn?
opportunityEditor.expectedDeliverablesDescription: Please describe the deliverables that are expected of the Capstone Team at the completion of the project (i.e. survey or evaluation instruments, draft policies and procedures, resource lists, analysis of large datasets, a comprehensive report, recommendations on how to proceed, etc.). Approximately 250 words.
opportunityEditor.fieldWorkLikelihood: How likely would the project require field work or site visits (either within NYC or outside)?

finalScope.banner: You've been accepted — describe the project scope below.
finalScope.scopeDescriptionHint: Describe the scope of the project for which you would like a Capstone Project Team to focus. (The best Capstone proposals are important but not urgent; achievable within the academic timeframe and with the resources available; provide a clear definition of the problem or issue to be addressed; have a realistic scope; and specify tangible deliverables for the Capstone team to provide.) Approximately 500 words.
finalScope.title: Project scope

followUpQuestionnaire.banner: You've been pre-selected — please complete the follow-up questions below.
followUpQuestionnaire.potentialActivitiesHint: Please describe potential activities in which the Capstone Team might engage (i.e. conduct a literature review, create and administer a survey, evaluate a program, analyze a dataset, etc.). 500 words maximum.
followUpQuestionnaire.specificSkillsHint: Specific skills or qualifications that would be helpful for the Capstone Team to have in order to address this problem (i.e. knowledge of specific computer software, statistics, experience in your organization's issue area, etc.). 500 words maximum.
followUpQuestionnaire.tbd: Follow-up questions will be added here.
followUpQuestionnaire.title: Follow-up questionnaire

opportunityEditor.guidelinesDescription: I have read and understood the Capstone proposal guidelines in full, including all of the Capstone Sponsor FAQs and Mutual Expectations agreement and agree to abide by them.
opportunityEditor.guidelinesRequestAppointment: I request an appointment to discuss further.
opportunityEditor.guidelinesWarning: Tick the guidelines checkbox before publishing.
opportunityEditor.introVideoHint: Upload a video file or paste an embed URL (YouTube, Vimeo). Uploaded files are stored on our cloud bucket.
opportunityEditor.introVideoUrlHint: Or paste a video URL (the URL itself — not the full <iframe> embed code). Supports YouTube, Vimeo, Loom, Google Drive, or a direct .mp4/.webm link. Maximum upload size: {{maxMb}} MB.
opportunityEditor.issueRelevanceDescription: Why is this challenge of particular relevance to your organization? What are the implications of this issue or problem for your agency? 100 words maximum.
opportunityEditor.learningOutcomesDescription: Provide 2-3 learning outcomes or expected deliverables. For example, an interactive dashboard or a website showcasing data analysis outcomes. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
opportunityEditor.mentorNotesDescription: Name, title, contact, and availability of the day-to-day mentor. If no mentor is available, describe the gaps and CUSP can help identify support.
opportunityEditor.offeredInNetworksHint: Pick which networks can see this opportunity.

overview.additionalNotes: Please describe any additional considerations the CUSP capstone team should be aware of, if applicable.
overview.additionalNotesHint: This may include data privacy restrictions and security requirements, NDA agreements, IRB approval, data access limitations, stakeholder coordination, or other constraints.
overview.anticipatedObstacles: Do you anticipate any obstacles the capstone student team might encounter while working on this project?
overview.anticipatedObstaclesHint: This may include issues such as incomplete datasets or datasets requiring significant cleaning; limited stakeholder buy-in; organizational changes or staffing transitions. (100 words)
overview.datasetProvision: How will the sponsoring organization provide datasets to the student team?

datasetProvisionOptions.orgNoRestrictions: Datasets will be provided directly by the organization to students by September 2026 and are not subject to privacy restrictions, nor do they require significant pre-processing/cleaning.
datasetProvisionOptions.orgPrivacyNda: Datasets will be provided directly by the organization to students by September 2026 and will require special privacy restrictions and/or NDA agreements in advance of the data transfer.
datasetProvisionOptions.public: Datasets for this project are publicly available.

overview.expectedDeliverables: Please identify the potential deliverables expected from the Capstone student team in May 2027. Select all that apply.

fieldResearchOptions.beneficial: Travel or site visits may be beneficial but are not required.

overview.fieldResearchRequired: Will the capstone student team be expected to conduct field research, site visits, or travel to locations beyond the primary client site to complete the project?
overview.fieldResearchTravelDetails: If yes, please describe the anticipated locations and any associated travel requirements.

hardwareOptions.teamDiscretion: The choice of hardware is at the discretion of the student team; there are no specific requirements for this project.

overview.internshipInterest: If not selected, would you be interested in converting your application as an unpaid or paid internship?
overview.projectDescriptionHint: Please describe your proposed project (goals and intended outcomes) including the challenge(s) it addresses. (250 words)
overview.projectTitleHint: Please limit to 15 words or fewer; shorter titles are preferred.
overview.relevanceHint: Please describe how this project is well-suited to CUSP's urban focus and leverages the skills of the M.S. in Urban Data Science cohort. (250 words)
overview.requiredHardware: What hardware, beyond a computer, is required for this project?
overview.requiredHardwareHint: Select all that apply; sponsoring organizations must provide any required hardware.
overview.requiredSoftware: Which of the following software or libraries are required for this project?
overview.requiredSoftwareHint: Select all that apply. Please note that sponsoring organizations must provide any non-open-source software not otherwise available to NYU students.
overview.requiresSpecialResources: Does this project require any specialized hardware, software, or other resources not otherwise available through NYU?
overview.requiresSpecialResourcesHint: For example, Raspberry Pi devices or specialized sensors. A list of software packages available to NYU students can be found here.

softwareOptions.teamDiscretion: The choice of software and technical tools is at the discretion of the student team; there are no specific requirements for this project.

overview.specialResourcesNotes: If yes, please describe the hardware, software, or other resources your organization will provide.
overview.title: Overview of Capstone Project Proposal

specialConsiderations.anticipatedObstaclesHint: Can you anticipate obstacles that a Capstone Team might encounter while working on this project (i.e. incomplete data, lack of buy-in for the project, staff turnover, etc.)? 250 words maximum.
specialConsiderations.designedForStudents: Designed with specific NYU CUSP students in mind?
specialConsiderations.designedForStudentsHint: If yes, please list student names. 15 words maximum.
specialConsiderations.fieldResearchTravelHint: Will the Capstone Team be required to conduct field research and/or visit multiple locations, other than the primary client site, to complete their project? Where would the team travel? 250 words maximum.
specialConsiderations.generalHint: If applicable, describe anything the Capstone Project Team should be aware of.
specialConsiderations.intro: If applicable, please describe any special considerations that NYU Wagner and/or the Capstone Project Team should be aware of.
specialConsiderations.privateClientDataHint: Will the Capstone Team have access to private client data that may present obstacles for progress on the project, and how/when do you plan to have approval for the Capstone team to proceed with gathering information given potential obstacles? 100 words maximum.
specialConsiderations.requiresBusinessHoursHint: Must a significant portion of research and data gathering be done during regular business hours? (Many students work full-time and may have constraints on their daytime availability). 50 words maximum.
specialConsiderations.title: Special considerations

opportunityEditor.sponsorIsMentor: I'm also the day-to-day mentor for this project (uncheck if someone else will be mentoring)
